title: The Happiness Equation
description: What really makes a country happy? An interactive exploration of the World Happiness Report.

story.text:
**The Happiness Equation**

What really makes a country happy? Every year, the World Happiness Report ranks nations by how their citizens rate their own lives. Let's explore what drives these scores.

Each dot is a country. The higher it sits, the happier its people report being. The further right, the wealthier the nation.

Notice the pattern? Richer countries tend to be happier — but the relationship isn't as simple as it looks.

**Money helps — but only up to a point.**

The curve flattens at the top. Qatar and Luxembourg are among the world's richest, but they don't top the happiness rankings. The Nordic countries do — with less wealth but stronger social fabric.

**The American paradox.**

The United States is one of the wealthiest nations on Earth, yet Costa Rica — with a fraction of the GDP — scores nearly as high on happiness. What's Costa Rica's secret?

**Latin America's secret: social support.**

When we switch the X-axis to social support, Latin American countries punch well above their weight. Strong family bonds and community ties create happiness that money can't buy.

**The real formula.**

Happiness isn't one thing — it's a cocktail of factors: GDP, social support, health, freedom, generosity, and low corruption. The happiest countries score well across all of them.

**Now it's your turn.**

Swap the axes. Filter by region. Click any country to see its full breakdown. Use the time slider to watch how happiness has changed since 2005.
